---
title: "title_block"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd) | [View source](https://codeberg.org/usrbinr/stwd/src/branch/main/R/stwd.R#L406)

# `title_block()`

Create a Title Block Plot

## Usage

```r
title_block(
  title,
  title_size = 12,
  halign = "left",
  lineheight = 1.1,
  margin_top = 5,
  margin_bottom = 5,
  margin_left = 5,
  margin_right = 5,
  width = 0.95,
  wrap_width = NULL,
  ...
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `title` | Main title text (marquee-formatted). Supports markdown formatting: **bold**, *italic*, {#color text. |
| `title_size` | Size for title in pts. Default: 12. |
| `halign` | Horizontal alignment: "left", "center", or "right". Default: "left". |
| `lineheight` | Line height multiplier. Default: 1.1. |
| `margin_top` | Top margin in pts. Default: 5. |
| `margin_bottom` | Bottom margin in pts. Default: 5. |
| `margin_left` | Left margin in pts. Default: 5. |
| `margin_right` | Right margin in pts. Default: 5. |
| `width` | Maximum width for text wrapping (0-1). Default: 0.95. |
| `wrap_width` | Character width to wrap text at. Default: NULL (no wrapping). |
| `...` | Additional arguments passed to `marquee::geom_marquee()`. |

## Value

A ggplot object that can be combined with other plots via patchwork.

## Description

Creates a standalone title element as a ggplot for use in patchwork
compositions.

## Details

Allows for larger, more prominent titles than standard ggplot titles.
Follows the SWD style where titles are bold statements that tell the
story, not just describe the chart. Supports marquee markdown:
**bold**, *italic*, and {#hexcolor text for colored text.

Use with patchwork's `/` operator to stack above your chart, and
`plot_layout(heights = ...)` to control proportions.

## Examples

```r
## Not run: 
library(patchwork)

title_plot <- title_block(
    "**Q4 Sales: {#E63946 Apples} Take the Lead**",
    title_size = 18
)

title_plot / my_chart + plot_layout(heights = c(0.08, 0.92))

## End(Not run)
```
